request.system:
You are a regulatory analyst. Given a prediction-market contract and a single regulatory news record, decide if this record is relevant to whether the contract resolves YES or NO. Score relevance 0-10 (0 = off-topic, 10 = directly determinative). Return JSON matching the schema. one_line_why should be ≤160 chars and explain how this record moves resolution probability.

Direction: judge whether this event makes the contract MORE likely to resolve YES (= bullish), LESS likely to resolve YES (= bearish), or has no directional effect (= neutral). IMPORTANT: direction is always relative to YES resolution. If your one_line_why says the event 'increases odds' or 'raises probability', direction MUST be bullish. If it says 'decreases odds' or 'lowers probability', direction MUST be bearish. Do not contradict yourself.

Magnitude: high = materially changes probability, medium = notable but not decisive, low = incremental signal.

Timeline shift: does this event suggest resolution will come sooner or later than expected, or no change (none)?
request.user:
Contract title: Will the CLARITY Act be signed into law in 2026?
Resolution criteria: This market will resolve to 'Yes' if the Digital Asset Market Clarity Act of 2025
(H.R.3633) is passed by both chambers of the U.S. Congress and signed into law by
December 31, 2026, 11:59 PM ET. Otherwise, this market will resolve to 'No'.

The primary resolution source will be official information from Congress.gov, the
White House, and the U.S. Government Publishing Office. A consensus of credible
reporting will also be used.

The CLARITY Act (also referred to as the Digital Asset Market Clarity Act) establishes
a regulatory framework distinguishing digital commodities from digital securities,
granting the CFTC primary jurisdiction over digital commodity spot markets and
clarifying the SEC's jurisdiction over digital asset securities.

Conditions:
  A: H.R.3633 passed & signed by 12/31/26 ET — H.R.3633 (Digital Asset Market Clarity Act of 2025) passes both House and Senate and is signed into law by Dec 31, 2026, 11:59 PM ET, confirmed by Congress.gov, White House, or GPO.
Settlement entities: ['U.S. House of Representatives', 'U.S. Senate', 'Commodity Futures Trading Commission', 'U.S. Securities and Exchange Commission', 'White House', 'Department of the Treasury']

Record title: In 2025, the PCAOB staff gathered insights from more than 250 audit committee chairs. A new staff Spotlight presents high-level observations and takeaways from this engagement. Inspections
Record date: 2026-04-27
Record regulator: Public Company Accounting Oversight Board
Record topic: Public Company Accounting Oversight Board
Record entities: ['BDO International Limited', 'Demetrios (Jim) Logothetis', 'Deloitte Touche Tohmatsu Limited', 'Ernst & Young Global Limited', 'Grant Thornton International Limited', 'KPMG International Cooperative', 'PricewaterhouseCoopers International Limited', 'Public Company Accounting Oversight Board', 'Securities and Exchange Commission']

response.one_line_why: PCAOB staff report on audit-committee outreach is unrelated to H.R.3633's legislative progress or Presidential signing, so no effect on outcome.
